$comment: Curated shadcn-compatible component registries. NONE are added by default. The setup script walks through each one and asks the user to opt in. All URLs below were verified against each library's official docs on 2026-04-21.

foundation.$comment: Always installed when missing. Not registries — the base shadcn CLI + two taste/motion skills.

ui-ux-pro-max.install: npm install -g uipro-cli && uipro init --ai claude
ui-ux-pro-max.notes: Design brain — 161 rules, 67 UI styles, 161 palettes, 57 font pairings. Runs before shadcn MCP to produce requirements briefs in plain design language.

emil-motion.notes: Motion/interaction refinement. Invoked only when requirements contain motion cues.

optional.$comment: Full catalog of shadcn-compatible registries. None installed by default — user picks per project. All URLs verified against official docs.

@magicui.name: Magic UI
@magicui.best_for: Animated / marketing components — animated beam, marquee, orbiting icons, particle background, gradient blob, icon cloud, bento grid, word rotator, spotlight, browser frame.

@aceternity.name: Aceternity UI
@aceternity.best_for: Motion-heavy hero sections, 3D cards, infinite scroll, timeline, sparkle effects, animated lists.
@aceternity.notes: Some components require extra deps (framer-motion, three.js). shadcn CLI prompts at add time.

@reui.name: REUI
@reui.best_for: Composable primitives + data-table / form patterns with strong defaults.
@reui.notes: REUI URLs take a style segment ('default' is the main one). If a component is published under a different style, change 'default' in the URL accordingly.

@smoothui.name: SmoothUI
@smoothui.best_for: Polished micro-interactions, card hover states, buttons with built-in motion.

@cardcn.name: Cardcn
@cardcn.best_for: Card-focused compositions — stat cards, feature cards, testimonial cards, pricing cards.
@cardcn.notes: No official registry docs — URL points at the GitHub raw file path inside the repo.

@shadcnstudio.name: ShadcnStudio
@shadcnstudio.best_for: 700+ visual-composer blocks built on shadcn base — landing pages, dashboards, full-section compositions.
@shadcnstudio.notes: ShadcnStudio also ships an MCP server — the setup script will prompt for its install command if you opt in.

@efferd.name: Efferd
@efferd.best_for: Community-submitted shadcn registry — eclectic mix of components.

@cultui.name: Cult UI
@cultui.best_for: Opinionated, trend-forward components — bento grids, glass cards, novel patterns.
@cultui.notes: MCP package name is a best guess — confirm at the docs URL before opt-in. Setup script will warn if the MCP install fails.

@kokonut.name: Kokonut UI
@kokonut.best_for: Polished micro-interactions, buttons, badges, form fields with built-in animation.

@tremor.name: Tremor
@tremor.best_for: Data dashboards — charts, KPI cards, sparklines, filtered tables.
@tremor.install_hint: npm install @tremor/react   (then import components directly — NOT a shadcn registry)
@tremor.notes: Tremor is an npm package, not a shadcn registry. shadcn MCP cannot search it. The setup script will flag this clearly and skip the components.json merge. Listed here because it's still a great charts/dashboard library — you just install it the traditional npm way.

components_json_template.$comment: The registries block merged into the project's components.json when a user opts in. Only opted-in entries with a real URL are written.

compatibility_profiles.$comment: Per-project registry compatibility overrides. Read by ui-workflow skill at Phase 3 Step 3 to auto-filter incompatible registries before search. Key = project identifier (matches project root folder name). Each entry defines which registries are allowed, their compatibility verdict, and for SELECTIVE registries, the specific components that pass the filter.

arel-dashboard.$comment: Arel OS Dashboard — Notion-minimal aesthetic. Forbidden: gradients on chrome, glassmorphism, backdrop-blur (without functional reason), cinematic/parallax effects, infinite animations on primary content. Reference: DESIGN-SYSTEM.md + COMPONENT-CATALOG-FILTERED.md

@shadcn.notes: Neutral baseline primitives. Always the default source. Use for every fundamental component.

@reui.notes: Extended shadcn. Same philosophy, 1003+ variants. Use when @shadcn doesn't cover a need: DataGrid, Kanban, Stepper, Filters, Sortable, Tree, Autocomplete, Phone Input, Number Field, Rating, Timeline.

@shadcnstudio.notes: Visual variant presets over shadcn primitives. Cherry-pick specific variant configurations — not a runtime dependency to add wholesale.

@smoothui.drop_reason: Drop: all text animations (Typewriter, Reveal Text, Scramble, Wave Text, etc.), all background effects (Gravity Stars, Aurora Bars, Wave Background, Pixel Background), Siri Orb, Aurora Card, Blob Card, Cursor Follow, marketing blocks. Reason: violate chrome-disappears principle or introduce locked decorative aesthetics.

@cultui.drop_reason: Drop: all Hero variants (Dithering, Color Panels, Heatmap, Liquid Metal), Neumorph/Cosmic/Gradient/Border Beam buttons, Logo Carousel, Tweet Grid, all visual effects (Grid Beam, Fractal Grid, LightBoard, SVG Shapes, Shader Lens Blur), 3D Carousel, Distorted Glass, all text animations, Background Texture, Neumorph Eyebrow. Reason: opinionated marketing aesthetics, gradient rule violations, or glassmorphism.

@kokonut.drop_reason: Drop: all animated backgrounds (Beams Background, Background Paths, Flow Field, Shapes Hero), Liquid Glass (glassmorphism), all gradient buttons (Gradient Button, Magnet Button, Particle Button), all text animations (Scroll Text, Typing Text, Matrix Text, Glitch Text, Shimmer Text, Sliced Text, Swoosh Text), Morphic Navbar, Carousel Cards, Bento Grid, spotlight cards, mouse effect card, currency transfer card. Reason: glassmorphism, gradient rule violations, or locked decorative aesthetics.

@unlumen.drop_reason: Drop: Shimmer Skeleton (use @shadcn Skeleton instead), Glowing Badge (decorative glow), Scramble Text, Shimmering Text, Text Reveal, Velocity Highlight, Count Up (use @smoothui Number Flow instead), Cursor (decorative cursor replacement), Glow Button (glow effect), Magnetic Button (motion gimmick), Orbiting Skills (marketing animation), Pixel Background, Tilt Card, Tilt. Reason: decorative effects that violate chrome-disappears principle.

@magicui.drop_reason: Drop: ALL text animations (Text Animate, Typing Animation, Number Ticker, Animated Shiny Text, Animated Gradient Text, Text Reveal, Hyper Text, Word Rotate, Morphing Text, Spinning Text, Sparkles Text, Aurora Text, Line Shadow Text, Video Text, Text Highlighter, Text 3D Flip, Scroll Based Velocity, Blur Fade), ALL backgrounds (Flickering Grid, Animated Grid Pattern, Retro Grid, Ripple, Hexagon Pattern, Striped Pattern, Interactive Grid Pattern, Light Rays, Noise Texture, Warp Background), ALL decorative effects (Meteors, Particles, Confetti, Shine Border, Magic Card, Glare Hover), ALL buttons (Rainbow, Shimmer, Ripple, Shiny, Pulsating, Interactive Hover), Globe, World Map, Device Mocks (Safari/iPhone/Android), Tweet Card, Bento Grid, Avatar Circles, Orbiting Circles, Icon Cloud, Animated Theme Toggler, Smooth Cursor, Backlight, Cool Mode, Neon Gradient Card, Animated Circular Progress Bar, Pixel Image, Comic Text, Scroll Progress, Pointer. Reason: decorative animations, locked visual aesthetics incompatible with Notion-minimal, or gradient rule violations.

@aceternity.drop_reason: Almost entirely incompatible with Arel OS design system. Heavy parallax, 3D transforms, glassmorphism overtones, cinematic motion, chromatic gradients — all violate Notion-minimal + no-glassmorphism + no-chromatic-gradient rules. Possibly evaluate Code Block and Timeline on a per-case basis only if explicitly requested by Vish, not as a default search target.

@cardcn.drop_reason: Most card categories are WIP or planned. Very limited scope (cards only). Check last. Only relevant if a card-specific surface has a concrete need that @shadcn Card + custom composition can't cover.

@efferd.drop_reason: Marketing blocks ONLY. Zero individual component primitives. All 141 blocks (Auth, Hero, Features, Pricing, Header, Footer, CTA, Testimonials, Integrations, Logo Cloud, FAQs, Contact) are SaaS landing-page sections with no applicability to a productivity dashboard.
